content.problemStatement: Rectify the error in the code.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string name consisting of visible characters without spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the character at index 7 (0‑indexed) of the given string. If the string length is less than 8, print an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The string "CodeChef" has length 8, so the character at index 7 is 'f'.

solution.approach: The task is purely positional. For each test case we only need to check whether the string has at least eight characters. If it does, the character at position 7 (0‑based) is printed; otherwise an empty line is printed. No additional data structures are required, making the solution O(|name|) per test case (just to read the string) and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) – linear in the input size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space besides the input string storage.
